title: rosa canina subvar. glaberrima
intro: Rosa canina subvar. glaberrima, also known as the hairless or glabrous dog rose, is a subspecies of the Rosa canina plant native to Europe and North Africa. It is a thorny shrub rich in medicinal properties, particularly its hips, which are a rich source of vitamin C, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds. The hips of Rosa canina subvar. glaberrima have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, inflammation, and colds, due to their antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The plant's hips can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or syrup, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for maintaining overall health and well-being.
